generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components //
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot, implementing adaptive control algorithms to mitigate nonlinear effects like friction within the safety constraints of [-90°, 90°] and maximum speed of 75°/s."
]

instance SafetyCell : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Safety mechanism restricting the maximum working range of the robotic system to [-90°, 90°] and limiting maximum speed to 75°/s to prevent operational errors."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance PrimaryMotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Rotary encoder mounted directly on the PMSM motor providing positional accuracy of 0.1 mm for high-precision motion tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PrimaryMotorPositionData
]

instance SecondaryGearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Rotary encoder positioned behind the gearbox providing redundant positional accuracy of 0.1 mm for high-precision motion tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryGearboxPositionData
]

instance JointCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor integrated with the Siemens SINUMERIK controller measuring motor input currents for joint friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData JointCurrentData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance PrimaryMotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PrimaryMotorPositionEncoder
]

instance SecondaryGearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryGearboxPositionEncoder
]

instance JointCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointCurrentSensor
]

instance AngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PrimaryMotorPositionEncoder
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PhysicsBasedDynamicSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink-based white-box model implementing analytical formulations of physical phenomena for interpretable behavior analysis of the robotic system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkExecutionEngine
]

instance DataDrivenFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Machine learning-based black-box model capturing nonlinear relationships like robotic joint friction using scalable identification methods."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABDataProcessing
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML IBD representing component-connector architecture of the robotic system defining interfaces, interconnections, and composition at functional/logical layers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance MontiArcAutomaton : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model using MontiArc automaton for detailed temporal dynamics of robotic system operations integrated with simulation tools."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcSimulationEngine
]

instance JointFrictionDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces of input currents and output angles for real-time friction detection and motion optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData JointCurrentData, AngularVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingComponent
]

instance OperationalPerformanceMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed performance metrics derived from sensor data traces including torque, velocity, and positional accuracy for operational analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PrimaryMotorPositionData, SecondaryGearboxPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingComponent
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SimulinkExecutionEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment enabling executable simulation models of the robot's dynamics with co-simulation capabilities for real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:enables JointFrictionMonitoringService, MotionOptimizationService
]

instance MATLABDataProcessing : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB environment for data replay, analysis, and friction detection using historical sensor data traces."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML tool for creating functional and logical architecture models including Internal Block Diagrams representing robotic components and connectors."
    DTDFVocab:enables ArchitectureDesignService
]

instance MontiArcSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc framework for executing behavioral automaton models with integration to simulation tools for temporal dynamics analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralSimulationService
]

instance MQTTDataStreaming : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time streaming of sensor data from Siemens SINUMERIK controller to the digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
]

instance DataProcessingComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component implementing adaptive algorithms for data trace aggregation and abstraction to derive actionable insights."
    DTDFVocab:enables PerformanceAnalysisService, DiagnosticSupportService
]

// C6: Services
instance JointFrictionMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time analysis service detecting friction loss in robotic joints through current-angle data analysis using both physics-based and data-driven models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionDetectionInsight
]

instance MotionOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service optimizing robotic motion by analyzing friction patterns and suggesting trajectory adjustments based on simulation results."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionOptimizationInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing historical sensor data traces to detect friction patterns and provide insights for maintenance planning."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceDiagnosticInsight
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating real-time sensor data streams for analysis and visualization purposes."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalVisualizationInsight
]

instance ArchitectureDesignService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing SysML-based architecture modeling for component-connector perspectives of the robotic system."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemArchitectureInsight
]

instance BehavioralSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing MontiArc automaton models for detailed temporal dynamics analysis of robotic operations."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemporalBehaviorInsight
]

instance PerformanceAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing operational performance metrics to identify degradation patterns and efficiency losses."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceDiagnosticInsight
]

instance DiagnosticSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Passive diagnostic service providing post-hoc analysis of robotic behavior for maintenance planning and process optimization."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceRecommendationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of friction loss in robotic joints based on current-angle data analysis using both physics-based and machine learning models."
]

instance MotionOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized motion trajectories suggested by the system to compensate for detected friction and improve operational efficiency."
]

instance OperationalVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of operational relationships and performance degradation patterns derived from aggregated sensor data."
]

instance SystemArchitectureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "SysML-based architectural representation showing component-connector relationships and functional/logical layer composition."
]

instance TemporalBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detailed temporal dynamics analysis of robotic operations using MontiArc automaton models integrated with simulation tools."
]

instance PerformanceDiagnosticInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive analysis of operational performance metrics identifying efficiency losses and degradation patterns."
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Post-hoc maintenance recommendations based on historical data analysis to prevent operational failures."
]

// C12: Environment
instance RoboticManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Advanced manufacturing environment incorporating the MABI MAX 100 six-axis robotic system for precision machining tasks."
    base:contains PhysicalRobotSystem, DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance PhysicalRobotSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Six-axis articulated robotic arm with integrated PMSM actuators, position encoders, and current sensors for high-precision motion control."
    base:isContainedIn RoboticManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital representation of the MABI MAX 100 robot integrating heterogeneous models and data sources for analysis and optimization."
    base:isContainedIn RoboticManufacturingEnvironment
    base:contains SimulinkExecutionEngine, MATLABDataProcessing, DataProcessingComponent, SysMLModelingTool, MontiArcSimulationEngine, MQTTDataStreaming
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the documents emphasizes **temporal synchronization** through structured data trace aggregation and abstraction, where **data traces** (contextual information from physical systems) are collected at varying granularities depending on the use case. While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms, or latency requirements) are not detailed, the system leverages **processing components** (e.g., simulation or data calculation models in tools like MathWorks Simulink or general-purpose languages like C++/Java) to compute higher-level abstractions from input data traces. These components ensure **traceability** of data origins, enabling real-time or near-real-time alignment between physical and digital states. The temporal granularity is implicitly governed by the **purpose-driven design** of the Digital Shadow Reference Model, where data traces are aggregated for specific objectives (e.g., manufacturing monitoring in injection molding), implying adaptive time scales based on stakeholder needs. No explicit mention of centralized time synchronization protocols (e.g., NTP, PTP) or strict latency constraints is provided in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s **orchestration and coordination** are framed within the **Digital Shadow Reference Model**, which defines a **processing-centric architecture** where **processing components** (e.g., simulation or data calculation models) consume input data traces and produce output data. This architecture follows a **component-based design** (e.g., using ADLs like SysML IBDs or AADL) to model systems as interconnected components with clear interfaces and interconnections. The **concretization** of the reference model allows for customization of orchestration patterns, such as integrating **heterogeneous tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, C++, or Java-based simulations) to handle behavior modeling. Resource management is implied through the **traceability** of data traces to their originating processing components, ensuring accountability and modularity. However, the documents do not specify explicit orchestration frameworks (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) or system-wide coordination mechanisms (e.g., API gateways, message brokers) beyond the **one-way data flow** characteristic of Digital Shadows. The **reference model’s conformance relation** ensures alignment between conceptual and concrete implementations, but detailed coordination protocols (e.g., REST, gRPC) are not mentioned."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system, as implied by the provided documents, is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which decompose system modeling into hierarchical abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. These layers are iteratively refined using **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**, **ACME, AADL, MontiArc**, or **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which describe components, interfaces, connectors, and composition. Transition management between stages involves **conforming models** derived from **reference models** (e.g., Digital Shadow Reference Model), where a **concrete realization** (concretization) incarnates abstract concepts into specific elements via **class diagrams (CDs)** with defined associations. The lifecycle governance is implicitly supported by **traceability mechanisms**—e.g., linking data traces to processing components, input traces, and models—ensuring consistency across stages. Metadata aggregation and abstraction (e.g., for simulation or data calculation models) further enable dynamic lifecycle adaptation. No explicit lifecycle governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are mentioned, but the process aligns with **purpose-driven design** (e.g., injection molding use cases) and **customizable reference model concretization** as described in [42]–[43]."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The fidelity and validity of the Digital Twin system are ensured through:
- **Model fidelity requirements**:
  - **White-box models** (e.g., SysML/Simulink-based) provide interpretable, physics-driven representations for predictable system behavior [58].
  - **Black-box models** (e.g., machine learning) capture nonlinear phenomena (e.g., robot friction) not amenable to analytical solutions [58].
  - **Combined paradigms** (white-box + black-box) are emphasized to balance interpretability and accuracy [58], [59].

- **Validation and verification methods**:
  - **Traceability**: Ensures correctness of transformations between modeling notations (e.g., SysML to simulation models) [33], [34].
  - **Verification**: Relies on **model-based development** techniques to validate data flows and component interactions [31].
  - **Uncertainty quantification**: Implicit in the aggregation of contextual data traces (e.g., sensor noise, model parameter drift) [32].

- **Error detection and correction mechanisms**:
  - **Active DT components** process incoming data to detect anomalies (e.g., sensor drift, model deviations) [35]–[37].
  - **Adaptive control** (e.g., probabilistic latent variable models) is referenced as a method to refine models dynamically (though specifics like implementation are not provided) [14], [58].

- **Quality assurance processes**:
  - **Purpose-driven data aggregation** (e.g., digital shadows for friction modeling) ensures relevance to industrial challenges [56], [57].
  - **Scalable identification methods** (e.g., leveraging intrinsic robot models) avoid dedicated setups and improve reliability [14], [58].
  - **No explicit mention of formal validation protocols** (e.g., ISO 15504, DO-178C) or statistical testing (e.g., Monte Carlo simulations).
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical network infrastructure for this system is defined by a **unidirectional communication flow** from physical sensors to the digital shadow, primarily relying on the **MQTT protocol** (as referenced in [52]) for data streaming. The network topology is not explicitly detailed, but the system assumes **real-time or low-latency requirements** for manufacturing applications (e.g., CNC machines [57]). Reliability mechanisms are not specified, though the passive nature of the digital shadow implies no active feedback loops or bidirectional control. Security protocols are not mentioned in the provided documents. Bandwidth and latency requirements are not explicitly quantified, but the use of MQTT suggests lightweight, publish-subscribe communication suitable for IoT environments. The system does not describe edge-cloud architectures or redundancy mechanisms beyond the passive aggregation of data traces."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The deployment infrastructure for this system is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents. However, the use of **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** (implied by [57] for manufacturing contexts) suggests a cloud-based or centralized hosting model for visualization and data aggregation. Computational and storage requirements are not specified, nor are scalability mechanisms (e.g., containerization, HPC, or NoSQL databases). The system does not mention on-premise or edge deployment models. Availability and reliability assurances are not addressed beyond the passive aggregation of data traces. References to open-source platforms (e.g., ThingsBoard [53]) or frameworks (e.g., Angular [54]) are not cited in the provided documents and thus cannot be included."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems through **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)**, a standardized interoperability framework for Industry 4.0, enabling seamless data exchange and semantic interoperability. Data is transmitted via **MQTT**, a lightweight protocol supporting **streamed, real-time communication** with low overhead. While the documents do not explicitly detail **enterprise system integration patterns** (e.g., OPC UA, REST APIs) or **cross-twin coordination**, the use of AAS implies adherence to **industry standards** for asset modeling and digitalization. The **Processing component** may rely on external data sources or services, but the scope of integration is primarily constrained to **purpose-driven data aggregation** and **simulation-based abstractions** rather than broader enterprise or multi-twin ecosystems."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system leverages **MQTT-based communication** (as referenced in [52]) for streaming sensor data, ensuring low-latency and reliable transmission of real-time operational metrics. Within the **RAMI 4.0 framework** ([49], [50]), the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** provides a structured approach to **safety-critical system considerations**, though explicit details on cybersecurity protocols or access control mechanisms are not documented. The system incorporates **physical safety cells** ([44], [42]) to mitigate risks in robotic operations, but no formal **threat mitigation strategies** (e.g., encryption, intrusion detection) or **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., emergency stop protocols) are specified beyond the general context of **digital shadows** ([42]) for automation pyramid levels. **Functional Mockup Interface (FMI)** ([51]) is referenced for model interoperability, but its role in safety-critical validation is not elaborated. The provided documents do not detail **authentication systems**, **risk assessment methodologies**, or **compliance with cybersecurity standards** (e.g., IEC 62443) beyond the implicit requirement for **traceable data origins** ([42])."
]
